title: Improve UPI Payment QR Code Scannability with Fallback

requirements.summary: Generate QR code using properly formatted UPI deep link for universal UPI app compatibility
acceptanceCriteria: The QR code encodes a valid upi://pay?pa=...&pn=...&am=...&cu=INR URI
Scanning the QR with Google Pay opens a pre-filled payment screen
Scanning the QR with PhonePe opens a pre-filled payment screen
Scanning the QR with BHIM opens a pre-filled payment screen

file_operations.description: Update the QR code generation to encode a properly formatted UPI deep link in the format: upi://pay?pa=<UPI_ID>&pn=<PAYEE_NAME>&am=<AMOUNT>&cu=INR. Replace any existing QR code data with this standardized format. Ensure the UPI ID, payee name, and amount are correctly URL-encoded and that the currency is set to INR. The QR library (likely qrcode.react or similar) should encode this string directly as the value prop.

requirements.summary: Upgrade QR code rendering with high error correction, quiet zone, and optimal resolution for crisp scanning
acceptanceCriteria: QR code uses error correction level H
QR is rendered with a white quiet zone border on all sides
QR modules are high contrast black on white
QR is rendered at minimum 256x256 pixels

file_operations.description: Upgrade the QR code component props to set errorCorrectionLevel='H' (highest damage tolerance), size to at least 256 pixels (e.g., 300 or 320 for better clarity), includeMargin={true} for automatic quiet zone border, and ensure the QR uses high contrast (default black on white). If using qrcode.react, apply these props directly to the QRCodeSVG or QRCodeCanvas component. Ensure the rendered QR has crisp, clear module separation suitable for mobile scanning.

requirements.summary: Add copyable UPI ID and tappable payment link fallback section below QR code with confirmation feedback
acceptanceCriteria: A clearly labeled fallback section appears below the QR code
The UPI ID is displayed with a copy button that copies it to clipboard
A 'Pay Now' or 'Open Payment App' link is shown that opens the upi:// deep link
Clicking copy shows a 'Copied!' confirmation message
The fallback section is visible on both mobile and desktop

file_operations.description: Below the QR code rendering section, add a new fallback UI labeled 'Can't scan? Use these instead:'. Display the UPI ID (e.g., extracted from the deep link) in a styled input or text field with a copy-to-clipboard button (use navigator.clipboard.writeText and show a brief 'Copied!' toast or inline message on success). Include a tappable anchor (<a> with href set to the full upi://pay?... deep link) labeled 'Pay Now' or 'Open Payment App' that triggers the user's default UPI app when clicked. Ensure the section is responsive and clearly visible on both mobile and desktop layouts.
